compiled_state.title: merge-to-main: ESCALATE signal -> agent continues problem-solving instead of stopping
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Observed behavior
After merge-to-main.sh fails 5 times and emits 'ESCALATE: Merge has failed 5 times. Stop and ask the user for help. Do NOT retry.', the agent continues attempting to problem-solve the merge issue instead of immediately stopping and surfacing the escalation to the user.

## Expected behavior
On receiving the ESCALATE signal from merge-to-main.sh, the agent must STOP all further tool calls and present the escalation to the user, asking for guidance. The ESCALATE message is an explicit stop instruction.

## Log evidence
Bash output: 'ESCALATE: Merge has failed 5 times. Stop and ask the user for help. Do NOT retry.'
Agent behavior: continued attempting to resolve the merge issue

## Root cause hypothesis
The ORCHESTRATOR_RESUME fence pattern (used to prevent premature stopping after sub-skill invocations) may be counteracting the ESCALATE signal, or the agent is not recognizing ESCALATE as a hard stop directive from a tool result.

## Impact
Lost time, potential for making merge conflicts worse by continued automated attempts.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — agent ignored ESCALATE hard-stop signal from merge-to-main.sh; Gate 1a skipped (CLI_user)
comments.author: Test
